title: A guide to 2026 World Cup Group F
original_text:
Japan has made it to the round of 16 four times in the team’s history. (Photo by Светлана Бекетова under CC BY-SA 3.0 .)

World Cup Group F gears up to play again June 20.

With billions of people watching the 2026 World Cup, the Netherlands, Sweden, Japan and Tunisia are going against each other in Group F’s first games. On June 14, the Netherlands faced Japan, resulting in a 2-2 tie. Sweden defeated Tunisia 5-1.

Group F’s next round of matches starts June 20. The Netherlands will play Sweden at 10 a.m. PT in Houston, and Japan will play Tunisia at 9 p.m. PT in Guadalupe, Mexico. The final round of matches in Group F will be played June 25. Japan will face Sweden at 4 p.m. PT in Arlington, Texas, and Tunisia will battle the Netherlands at 4 p.m. PT in Kansas City. After all of these teams play, the winner and runner-up of Group F will advance to the tournament’s knockout stage.

The Netherlands, ranked No. 8, are considered the favorites of this group, with strength across the field in defense, midfield and offense led by Virgil van Dijk, Frenkie de Jong, Memphis Depay and Cody Gakpo. Some players are injured, including defender Jurriën Timber with a groin injury and attacker Xavi Simons with a torn ACL. The injuries could hurt the Oranje’s chances of making it far, which could give some of the other teams a better chance to advance.

Dutch coach Ronald Koeman has led the team to two international semifinals — in 2023 at the Nations League and in 2024 at the Euros.

Van Dijk believes this team can go far in the tournament.

“We have a really tight-knit group, and I really believe in it,” Van Dijk told FIFA.

No. 35 Sweden is led by coach Graham Potter, who has coached at Chelsea, West Ham, Brighton and Swansea. Sweden returns to the tournament after not qualifying for the 2022 World Cup. One player to watch is striker Viktor Gyökeres, who plays for Arsenal. In the Premier League, Gyökeres has scored 14 goals and will likely be Sweden’s greatest asset.

This is No. 18 Japan’s eighth consecutive World Cup appearance. They are managed by Hajime Moriyasu, who was also the manager of the 2022 World Cup. The Samurai Blue have made it to the round of 16 four times in their history.

Wataru Endo, who plays for Liverpool, will not be playing due to a left foot injury. This was a tough decision that Moriyasu had to make since Endo was Japan’s team captain. Endo passed on his captain position to Ko Itakura, one of Japan’s defenders.

Every qualifying match Japan played was in preparation for the World Cup.

“Every match we played, we were thinking about how we’d be able to win against the world’s best teams,” Endo told FIFA.

No. 56 Tunisia was the first African country to win a World Cup game in 1978. While Tunisia lost in the group stage in 2022, they managed to defeat France, which reached the finals in 2022 — a massive accomplishment.

They are coached by Sabri Lamouchi, who is expecting to be fired after the loss to Sweden. Make sure to watch out for Elias Achouri and Elias Saad, both of whom scored three goals in the qualifying matches leading up to the World Cup and are key attacking players for Tunisia.

Filled with high-ranking teams, Group F is a challenging group to be placed in, but these games will certainly be exciting to watch.

Netherlands vs. Sweden at 10 a.m. PT in Houston, Texas

Tunisia vs. Japan at 9 p.m. PT in Guadalupe, Mexico

Japan vs. Sweden at 4 p.m. PT in Arlington, Texas

Tunisia vs. Netherlands at 4 p.m. PT in Kansas City, Missouri

Email notifications are only sent once a day, and only if there are new matching items.
